title: This is how Bad Bunny wrapped up his world tour in Brussels: "Why Belgium? Now I understand, even though it’s cold here"

environment.visibilityPolicy: quiet camera-pinned pale paper support from accepted world-environment; not a scenic or literal Brussels background

designFrame.subject: shared central fractured geometric plane stack
designFrame.cameraSkillTakeaway: canonical projection retained unless preview evidence proves desktop or mobile framing failure

outputColorTransform.gradingNotes: single renderer-owned output grade; not used as a substitute for part geometry readability

owns: neutral background or clear-space support when needed
avoid: stealing creative part ownership
turning the background into a mandatory visual claim
adding literal Brussels, concert, weather, or artist-reference imagery
using forbidden WebGPU surfaces

facts: support-only quietness is intentional
no dominant shell, skybox, or scenic container is required
environment background visibility is not required unless a later composition part independently needs it
visibleAbsence.rationale: The part exists to satisfy technical compatibility and spatial safety; a quiet or absent visible output prevents it from overwriting the artwork's central Youngerman-derived construction.

paramSemantics.scale: kept at native accepted scale to preserve preview-backed module geometry and camera envelope

avoid: using dark black as a broad ground
dense mark noise unrelated to edges
generic foreground mark field naming
overdrawing the large silhouettes
large CPU-baked procedural texture generation
photographic paper assets
raw shader strings
color gradients that weaken flat-poster-ink behavior
treating pale paper as mandatory full background

facts: matte and tactile surface behavior must visibly affect creative parts
the material system can be shared rather than a standalone object
visibleAbsence.rationale: The material strategy is a required construction carrier; it may be distributed across parts, but it cannot be visually absent.
